rationale_for_baseline_tier: This is anaphylaxis: typical skin features (widespread urticaria) PLUS persistent severe gastrointestinal symptoms PLUS circulatory involvement (presyncope/dizziness on standing, tachycardia HR 112, relative hypotension BP 105/65) after mammalian meat. Under ASCIA criteria, anaphylaxis with any cardiovascular involvement mandates intramuscular adrenaline and an ambulance. The absence of airway involvement does NOT downgrade this — circulatory anaphylaxis is itself life-threatening, no adrenaline is available on site, the process is evolving, and telehealth cannot monitor for rapid deterioration. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the honest tier. This is the genuine-acuity exception among Complex-Fatigue-Entity cases: the acute red-flag screen is positive, so the case is tiered to true acuity rather than floored low.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Current state — widespread hives + severe abdominal cramps + presyncope/dizziness on standing with a racing heart after eating red meat
escalation_edges.rationale: Multi-system anaphylaxis with circulatory involvement requires adrenaline and emergency ambulance without delay; paramedics carry adrenaline as none is available on site.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This looks like a serious whole-body allergic reaction affecting your circulation. Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them it may be anaphylaxis. Lie down flat and raise your legs — do not stand up or walk. If anyone can get an adrenaline autoinjector (EpiPen), use it into the outer thigh straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any throat tightness, lip/tongue swelling, difficulty breathing or swallowing, or wheeze develops
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately if it occurs — give/repeat adrenaline and keep 000 on the line
escalation_edges.rationale: Extension to the upper airway is the most rapidly fatal progression of anaphylaxis and mandates immediate adrenaline and emergency airway support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your throat starts to feel tight, your lips or tongue swell, or breathing or swallowing becomes hard — that is an emergency. Use an adrenaline autoinjector if one is available and make sure 000 knows straight away.

escalation_edges.rationale: Circulatory or respiratory arrest from anaphylaxis requires lying flat with legs raised and, if breathing stops, bystander CPR while paramedics respond.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he collapses, lay him flat and raise his legs, keep him warm, and tell 000 immediately — start CPR if he stops breathing and you know how.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: No adrenaline autoinjector or allergy medication available at home; patient has no prior known allergy so is not carrying one
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Because no autoinjector is on site, the primary lever is an immediate 000 ambulance (paramedics carry adrenaline) plus lying flat with legs raised; urgently ask whether any neighbour/relative has an in-date autoinjector, and if so use it into the outer thigh straight away.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance now and say it may be a severe allergic reaction (anaphylaxis) with dizziness and a racing heart after eating red meat. Lie down flat and raise the legs; do not stand or walk. If any in-date adrenaline autoinjector can be found, use it into the middle of the outer thigh (it works through clothing) and repeat after 5 minutes if there is no improvement. Stay together, unlock the front door for the paramedics, and watch for any throat tightness or trouble breathing.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a GP callback.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary and an ASCIA anaphylaxis action plan; take these to the GP/allergist for specific IgE (alpha-gal) testing and prescription of two in-date adrenaline autoinjectors.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the picture as simple hives, a mild allergy or food poisoning and advising home antihistamines, self-care or a routine GP appointment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Untreated circulatory anaphylaxis progresses to airway compromise and distributive shock without adrenaline or emergency care — potentially fatal — and a biphasic reaction may recur unmonitored.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Directing to same-day GP or urgent care only, delaying adrenaline and emergency monitoring for a reaction with active circulatory involvement.
